title: Share of one-year-olds vaccinated against measles
subtitle: Share of one-year-olds who have received the first dose of the measles vaccine (MCV1).
caption:
Share of one-year-olds vaccinated against measles, 2024
Share of one-year-olds who have received the first dose of the measles vaccine (MCV1).

This choropleth map displays the global distribution of measles vaccination coverage for one-year-olds in 2024, utilizing a continuous color scale to represent the percentage of the target population that has received the first dose (MCV1). The visualization reveals a distinct geographic stratification in immunization rates, with the highest coverage levels concentrated across the Americas, Europe, and East Asia. Countries such as the United States, Canada, Brazil, and Australia appear in the darkest blue band, indicating vaccination rates between 95% and 100%. Similarly, Russia and China show high coverage within the 90% to 95% range. In contrast, the lowest vaccination rates are visible across a broad swath of sub-Saharan Africa and parts of the Middle East. Nations including Chad, Nigeria, Somalia, and South Sudan fall into the lightest color bands, representing coverage between 0% and 60%. Intermediate values appear in regions such as Southeast Asia and parts of South America; for instance, Indonesia and Papua New Guinea display colors corresponding to the 60% to 80% range, while Mexico and Argentina sit in the 80% to 90% band. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching, denoting an absence of reported data for the year. The spatial arrangement shows that while high coverage is the norm in the Northern Hemisphere's developed economies and much of Latin America, significant gaps persist in central and western Africa, where the color scale consistently shifts toward the lower percentage thresholds. The map presents a clear divide between regions achieving near-universal coverage and those where a substantial portion of the one-year-old population remains unvaccinated.
